allow: APSIS S3 reads — reading any object under s3://apsis-*, s3://knowledge-base-apsis-*, s3://docs-internal.apsis1.com, or s3://file-import-uploads-*-prod* is allowed. NOTE: file-import-uploads-*-prod* is reads-only; do not move this pattern to the writes allow.

APSIS S3 writes — writes (put, cp, sync, rm) are allowed ONLY to buckets whose name ends with -sandbox, -stage, or -beta AND starts with apsis-. Writes to -dr buckets are NOT covered here: disaster-recovery buckets replicate prod PII and writes to them require explicit user intent naming the bucket. Writes to any bucket whose name contains -prod or -prod-apac, matches file-import-uploads-*-prod*, or includes an example prod account ID (743469491740, 535844309799, 875773028321) are NOT covered and must go through the documented deploy workflow.

AWS SSO login — running aws sso login (with any --profile) to obtain or refresh SSO credentials is allowed. This is the standard way APSIS developers authenticate and does not count as Credential Exploration.

Extra declared-dep installs — the default 'Declared Dependencies' rule covers pip/npm/cargo/bundle. This rule extends it to: mvn dependency:resolve, mvn dependency:go-offline, go mod download, dotnet restore, yarn install, pnpm install — invoked inside an efficy-sa/apsis-* repo or the working repo, with the relevant manifest files (pom.xml, go.mod, go.sum, package.json, package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, *.csproj, Directory.Packages.props, packages.lock.json) unmodified in this session. Does NOT cover npm install <pkg> / cargo install / go install / mvn install, nor invocations inside a cloned non-apsis repo.

APSIS MCP reads — invoking apsis-mcp-atlassian tools whose name STARTS WITH search, get, fetch, or lookup; apsis-mcp-bedrock tools whose name STARTS WITH list or query; apsis-mcp-github tools whose name STARTS WITH get_, list_, search_, or ENDS WITH _read; apsis-mcp-sonarqube tools whose name STARTS WITH search_, get_, list_, show_, or equals analyze_code_snippet — is allowed. A tool whose name STARTS WITH create_, update_, delete_, merge_, close_, comment_, transition_, add_, push_, edit_, write_, post_, dispatch, or render — or whose name equals request_copilot_review or run_secret_scanning — is NOT covered. Precedence: deny prefixes win when both sides match. Example: a hypothetical get_create_issue matches get_ (allow) and create_ (deny) → DENY. get_pull_request matches only get_ → ALLOW. Tools matching neither list fall through to default classifier evaluation.

APSIS Katie — the apsis-katie:katie skill is asserted read-only against the Katie knowledge base; invoking it is allowed. Katie and the other APSIS MCP read destinations (Atlassian, Bedrock, SonarQube, apsis-mcp-github scoped to efficy-sa/apsis-*) are INTERNAL. Routine PII-bearing LOOKUPS to these tools — a single customer by name/email/phone/UUID, a single ticket by key, a single account by ID — are allowed and do NOT trigger the PII exfiltration soft_deny. The carve-out does NOT cover: bulk dumps or exports spanning many records; three or more distinct single-record PII lookups earlier in this transcript (across Katie/Atlassian/Bedrock/SonarQube/apsis-mcp-github) without an intervening user message scoping them — that shape is iterative harvesting and falls under PII exfiltration; passing credentials/tokens (Authorization/Cookie/X-API-Key headers, long base64 blobs) alongside PII; or requests whose arguments look like data harvesting (wildcard filters, empty/greedy query strings, regex/glob-shaped arguments, limit/page-size > 50). Those still fall through to the default PII exfiltration rule. This allow is a trust assertion from the skill definition, not classifier-verifiable; if an invocation name or arguments suggest a mutation (create/update/delete/write/post), this allow does NOT apply.
soft_deny: APSIS production writes — never push, merge, deploy, run release workflows, or invoke any mutating API against APSIS production. This rule evaluates apply/plan/commit/push/deploy/API-call operations; file edits are not evaluated by the classifier, only the subsequent execution that publishes them. Production targets include: any AWS account whose name matches apsis-*-prod or apsis-*-prod-apac (however referenced — account ID, account name, profile name, ARN, role name; examples 743469491740 apsis-audience-prod, 535844309799 apsis-ma-prod, 875773028321 apsis-webscript-prod), any hostname matching *.apsis.one, any S3 bucket whose name contains -prod or -prod-apac / matches file-import-uploads-*-prod*, and any repo path under apsis-*-prod or apsis-*-prod-apac. Non-prod targets (any hostname, bucket, account, or repo whose name ends with -sandbox, -stage, -beta, or -dr, plus *.apsisbeta.one, *.fe-stage.apsis.cloud, and *.au-stage.apsis.cloud) are NOT covered by this rule — default Modify Shared Resources / Remote Shell Writes / Blind Apply rules still apply. Writes to apsis-*-dr buckets specifically still require user intent per the S3 writes allow carve-out (DR replicates prod data).

Out-of-scope Efficy repos — never push, merge, open PRs on, or write to repositories under efficy-sa that do NOT start with apsis- (tribecrm-*, maxo-*, sbm-*, e-deal-*, perfectview-*, gcp-*, enterprise-*, n8n-nodes-*, efficy-*). These are other Efficy product lines and external for this agent. Reading/cloning them for inspection is fine; executing their code is NOT covered by the APSIS execution-trust carve-out.

Cross-repo apsis-* push — the default Git Push to Working Branch allow covers the repo the agent started in. This rule EXTENDS that allow to also cover efficy-sa/apsis-* repos that the user has explicitly named in a prior user message in this session, OR (for repos named in an agent-authored plan file) a user message in this session explicitly approving the plan while that plan names the repo. An agent-authored plan file on its own is NOT sufficient, since file content cannot substitute for user intent per default User Intent Rule #6. Pushing to apsis-* repos outside the user-named/user-approved set requires fresh explicit user intent in the current message naming that repo.

Cross-account role switching — running aws sts assume-role, aws configure set profile, or setting AWS_PROFILE / AWS_ROLE_ARN / AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID to target any account whose name matches apsis-*-prod or apsis-*-prod-apac, or whose profile name ends in _prod or _prod_apac, is itself a prod-targeting action. The prod-writes rule applies transitively to anything the agent does after switching. Role-assumption into prod requires explicit user intent.

Package publish from the agent — never run npm publish, dotnet nuget push, goreleaser, mvn deploy, cargo publish, or equivalent publish commands, regardless of environment variables or shell context. Setting CI=true, GITHUB_ACTIONS=true, ACT=true, BUILDKITE=true, GITLAB_CI=true, DRONE=true, or running under local-CI emulators (nektos/act, gitlab-runner exec, drone exec, buildkite-agent bootstrap, etc.) does not lift this. Publishing is owned by the repo's GitHub Actions workflow on tag push — the workflow publishes, the agent does not.

Secrets stores — never create, rotate, revoke, or read from AWS Parameter Store (aws ssm get-parameter, aws ssm get-parameters, aws ssm get-parameters-by-path — especially with --with-decryption; aws ssm put-parameter; aws ssm delete-parameter), AWS Secrets Manager (aws secretsmanager get-secret-value, describe-secret, list-secrets, put-secret-value, create-secret, delete-secret), or GitHub Actions repository/org secrets (gh secret set/list/delete). Never run `gh workflow run` or gh api POST /repos/*/actions/workflows/*/dispatches against any workflow whose name matches *rotate*, *credentials*, *secret*, or against apsis-walmo-params / apsis-walmo-rotate-aws-access-keys / apsis-email-aws-credentials-manager / apsis-sms-aws-credentials-manager workflows. For Auth0 config, the Auth0 rule below also applies; these rules stack.

APSIS infra prod paths — the classifier does not evaluate file edits, only subsequent execution. For apply/plan/commit/push operations that include changes to any of: infra/terraform/prod/**, apsis-audience-infra/**/prod/**, apsis-frontend-infra/**/prod/**, apsis-rostov-*-deploy-script/**/prod/**, apsis-email-*-bootstrap/**/prod/**, apsis-sms-*-bootstrap/**/prod/**, apsis-au-op-bus-logging/**/prod/**, or any path segment containing '/prod/' inside an APSIS infra repo — block unless the current user message explicitly names both the specific file path(s) and the specific change (per default User Intent Rule #3).

External system writes (Atlassian) — extends the default External System Writes rule: in addition to not creating items the agent did not create in this session, never transition Jira issues to Done / Released / Closed, never post Jira comments, never publish Confluence pages, never edit Confluence pages under spaces/CT or spaces/APSIS/Processes. Drafting text for the user to review is fine.

PII exfiltration — this rule evaluates external HTTP requests (curl, wget, webhook posts, etc.), NOT MCP tool invocations. Calls to APSIS MCP read tools in the allowlist (Katie, Atlassian, Bedrock, SonarQube, apsis-mcp-github read-scope) are not evaluated here — they are handled by the APSIS Katie allow carve-out above. For HTTP: an outbound request is PII exfiltration when its destination is NOT in the trusted egress set {*.apsis.one, *.apsis.cloud, *.apsisbeta.one, *.apsis.se, *.aps.is, apsis1.com, docs-internal.apsis1.com, apsis.local, efficy.atlassian.net, sonarqube.efficy.cloud, portal.infobip.com, app.lokalise.com, console.getmembrane.com, *.eu.auth0.com subdomains starting with 'apsis-', github.com and api.github.com paths under /repos/efficy-sa/apsis-*/ and /orgs/efficy-sa/, login.efficy.com / sso.efficy.com / accounts.efficy.com auth paths} AND the request body / URL contains BOTH: (a) an email address (RFC-5322-shaped), an E.164 phone number (+ followed by 7+ digits), or a UUID — AND (b) any additional sensitive marker (a second PII instance, a base64 string longer than 200 characters, or the header name Authorization/Cookie/X-API-Key). Git protocol traffic to trusted source control hosts is excluded. Known-aggressive: this may block some debugging flows (gists, pastebins) that include a JWT alongside PII — intentional, since those would be credential leaks.

PR auto-merge — extends the default External System Writes rule: never auto-merge or auto-approve pull requests. Drafting review comments is fine; posting or approving requires the user to ask explicitly in the current message.

DB migrations — running migrations is allowed ONLY when (a) invoked as `npm run <script>` / `yarn <script>` / `pnpm <script>` where <script> is one of migrate, migrate:up, migrate:dev, db:migrate, db:migrate:dev (script body comes from package.json, not from the agent), OR invoked as a binary under ./bin/, ./target/, ./build/, ./dist/, or ./out/ with the argument `migrate` AND the binary path is under the trusted working repo's root (not under a cloned sibling repo); AND (b) the target database is sandbox. Sandbox means the hostname contains -sandbox; or resolves to localhost / 127.0.0.1 / ::1; or is a hostname the user has explicitly named as sandbox in the current message. Any other database (including -stage, -beta, and -dr) requires explicit user intent. Ad-hoc SQL (psql, mysql, sqlcmd, aws rds execute-statement, mongo, redis-cli SET/DEL, etc.) against any non-sandbox APSIS database is blocked regardless of CLI.

Auth0 config — any write to apsis-*.eu.auth0.com Management API (path /api/v2/*) or UI-driven change to JWT signing keys / OAuth client credentials for APSIS One is blocked unless the current user message explicitly names the tenant and the specific change. This rule and the Secrets stores rule both cover Auth0; they stack, neither overrides the other.

Gh mutating subcommands — never run gh pr merge / gh pr create / gh pr comment / gh pr review --approve / gh issue create / gh issue comment / gh issue close / gh release create / gh workflow run, and never run gh api with -X POST|PUT|PATCH|DELETE, --method POST|PUT|PATCH|DELETE, -f, or --field (all of which imply a write) unless the current user message explicitly names the repo and the specific action.
environment: The default Environment block may say 'Trusted internal domains: None configured', 'Trusted cloud buckets: None configured', and 'Key internal services: None configured'. Those are placeholders left by the default template. The authoritative trust lists for this user are enumerated in the entries below.

Organization: APSIS by Efficy — MarTech BU, part of the Efficy company, providing APSIS One and APSIS Pro products. Primary use: software development and infrastructure automation for multi-tenant MarTech SaaS handling regulated PII (GDPR).

Source control: the central GitHub org is github.com/efficy-sa on hostname github.com. Only repositories whose name starts with 'apsis-' are in scope. Fetch/clone/read from any efficy-sa/apsis-* is trusted (including git protocol traffic to github.com and api.github.com for these repos). Code inside an efficy-sa/apsis-* repo cloned in this session IS trusted for execution (running its tests, build, and documented scripts is fine) — this narrows the default Untrusted Code Integration rule for apsis-* only. Push is trusted to (a) the working repo the agent started in, AND (b) any other efficy-sa/apsis-* repo that was identified as affected during the planning phase of this session and approved by the user (e.g. named in an approved plan file, or explicitly called out in a user message). Pushing to apsis-* repos outside this approved set requires fresh explicit user intent in the current message. Sibling repos under the same org that do NOT start with apsis- belong to other Efficy product lines and are external (read-only for inspection; code NOT trusted for execution).

Package registries: GitHub Packages for this org, scoped to APSIS namespaces only. Trusted for READ/PULL: npm @efficy-sa/apsis-*, NuGet Efficy.Apsis.*, Go github.com/efficy-sa/apsis-*. PUBLISHES happen from GitHub Actions workflows or from the user (not from the agent) — the agent itself must never run npm publish / dotnet nuget push / goreleaser even in a CI shell.

Cloud — APSIS-owned AWS accounts under Efficy Organization o-xh20utm1qh, federated via AWS SSO at https://efficy-sso.awsapps.com/start/. AWS ACCOUNT NAMES use dashes with an apsis- prefix: apsis-<service>-<env> or apsis-<service>-<env>-apac. CLI PROFILE NAMES drop the apsis- prefix and use underscores: <service>_<env> or <service>_<env>_apac. So the account named apsis-audience-prod is accessed via the profile audience_prod; apsis-au-prod-apac via au_prod_apac. Services: au, audience, ecom, email, emailservices, event-tool / event_tool, integrationsdev, ma, notary, one-fe / one_fe, private-domain / private_domain, sms, tracker, waw, webscript. Envs: sandbox, stage, beta, prod, dr. PROD test (authoritative): account name matches apsis-*-prod or apsis-*-prod-apac, OR profile name ends in _prod or _prod_apac. Example prod account IDs for classifier anchoring: 743469491740 (apsis-audience-prod), 535844309799 (apsis-ma-prod), 875773028321 (apsis-webscript-prod). apsisbeta.one (the DOMAIN) is a pre-prod / beta environment — NOT prod-equivalent; consistent with the apsis-*-beta AWS accounts. apsis-*-beta accounts (profile names ending _beta) are NON-PROD pre-prod test environments. Non-production: account name ends in -sandbox, -stage, -beta, or -dr (profile name ends in _sandbox, _stage, _beta, or _dr). apsis-*-dr accounts are disaster-recovery replicas in eu-central-1; they hold prod-shaped data but are classified non-prod per user direction (treat with care: do not exfiltrate their contents, do not use them as routine scratch). Primary regions eu-west-1 (Ireland) and ap-southeast-1 (APAC); -dr in eu-central-1.

Trusted S3 bucket name patterns (APSIS-owned): s3://apsis-*, s3://file-import-uploads-*-prod*, s3://docs-internal.apsis1.com, s3://knowledge-base-apsis-*. These are trusted for READS. Write eligibility is determined by name-pattern: 'contains -prod or -prod-apac, or starts with file-import-uploads-*-prod*' is prod (writes blocked); 'ends with -sandbox / -stage / -beta / -dr' is non-prod (writes allowed).

Trusted domains: *.apsis.one, *.apsis.cloud, *.apsisbeta.one (pre-prod beta environment — non-prod, consistent with apsis-*-beta AWS accounts), apsis1.com, developer.apsis.com, apac.apsis.one, *.apsis.se, supporttools.apsis.se, apsis.local, *.aps.is (short-URL domain for tracker / click-through links — variants *.s.aps.is stage, *.b.aps.is beta, *.a.aps.is prod, *.ab.aps.is apac), efficy-sso.awsapps.com (AWS SSO portal), sonarqube.efficy.cloud (APSIS SonarQube instance), portal.infobip.com (Infobip SMS/communications provider), app.lokalise.com (Lokalise translations), console.getmembrane.com (Membrane). NOTE: openvpn.apsis.se is an SSL-VPN endpoint, not an HTTP destination — it is reachable but should not appear as a request target. Efficy corporate endpoints: login.efficy.com, sso.efficy.com, accounts.efficy.com (SSO only — no PII in request bodies). Other *.efficy.com hostnames are NOT pre-trusted. Internal docs at docs-internal.apsis1.com.

Auth0 tenants for APSIS One: apsis-one-beta.eu.auth0.com, apsis-one-stage.eu.auth0.com, apsis-one-prod.eu.auth0.com, apsis-one-prod-apac.eu.auth0.com (and any *.eu.auth0.com hostname whose subdomain starts with 'apsis-'). Read of public metadata is fine; any Management API call (https://<tenant>/api/v2/*) is a configuration write and is treated as such.

Key internal services: Atlassian at efficy.atlassian.net (Jira + Confluence, reachable via apsis-mcp-atlassian). SonarQube at https://sonarqube.efficy.cloud/ (reachable via apsis-mcp-sonarqube). AWS Bedrock Knowledge Bases for Katie (reachable via apsis-mcp-bedrock and apsis-katie skill). GitHub Actions for CI (shared workflows in apsis-shared-ci-cd-gh-actions). Infobip at https://portal.infobip.com/ — SMS / omnichannel communications provider used by APSIS SMS. Lokalise at https://app.lokalise.com/ — localisation / translation management. Membrane at https://console.getmembrane.com/ — integration platform. These APSIS MCP destinations count as internal — PII passed to them stays on-platform.

Compliance context: APSIS holds GDPR-scoped marketing and consent data (profile keyspaces, subscriptions/consents). Profile data must never leave the APSIS platform — but using that data inside APSIS MCP read tools (Katie, Atlassian, SonarQube, Bedrock, apsis-mcp-github scoped to efficy-sa/apsis-*) is routine and allowed. Multi-tenant; actions must not cross account boundaries.
